data.title: GE Vernova soars to all-time high after Q1 beat and raise; bookings surge to $18.3B
data.description:
GE Vernova (GEV) up 11.5% to an all-time high of $1,142 in early trading Wednesday after crushing expectations for Q1 earnings and raising revenue guidance for the full year, sparked by rising demand for its power and electrification solutions.

Q1 net profit skyrocketed to $4.75B, or $17.44/share, including a $3.99B pre-tax gain related to the Prolec GE acquisition, from $264M, or $0.91/share, in the year-earlier quarter, while revenues rose 16% Y/Y to $9.4B.

Q1 revenues jumped 12% Y/Y to $4.97B at the company's power unit and surged 61% to $2.96B at the electrification unit, although sales at the wind business fell 23% to $1.43B; orders increased 71% on an organic basis to $18.3B.

GE Vernova (GEV) raised FY 2026 guidance for revenues of $44.5B-$45.5B, up from its previous outlook of $44B-$45B; adjusted EBITDA margin of 12%-14%, up from 11%-13%; and free cash flow of $6.5B-$7.5B, up from $5B-$5.5B.

Analysts on average expect full-year revenues of $44.5B and adjusted EBITDA margin of 13.7%, according to data compiled by LSEG.

CEO Scott Strazik said the company ​expects to reach at least 110 GW of combined gas turbine ​backlog and slot reservation agreements by year-end.

GE Vernova (GEV) "delivered strong beat-and-raise results while continuing to show strong bookings," Oppenheimer analyst Colin Rusch said in a note. "Important in our view is that bookings in the quarter totaled $18.3B, a run rate that supports our 2030 revenue estimates, as we believe investors are now looking out to the end of the decade for valuation support."

data.title: NIO plans to showcase all three of its brands at the Auto China 2026 event
data.description:
NIO (NIO) updated on Wednesday that it will use Auto China 2026 to present the NIO, ONVO, and Firefly brands together for the first time at a major global auto show, under the theme "A Vast New Realm." The electric vehicle maker plans to display 11 models and 12 full-stack technologies.

In a LinkedIn post, NIO (NIO) highlighted that the showcasing of the three brands will signal a unified presence rather than separate product reveals. It also noted that Firefly will bring a refreshed model plus three custom builds, emphasizing design, powertrain, and smart-feature upgrades. The Auto China 2026 event is seen by the company as a milestone moment where it can show off the breadth of its portfolio, from premium EVs to lifestyle branding.

Auto China 2026 is the short name for the Beijing International Automotive Exhibition. The  biennial auto show will be held in Beijing from April 24 to May 3. It is being billed as the world’s largest auto show, with a record 380K square meters of exhibition space spread across two adjacent venues.

This year the show is focused on new energy vehicles, smart connectivity, autonomous driving, parts suppliers, and broader mobility technology. Organizers say it will bring together automakers, tech companies, suppliers, and industry stakeholders from China and abroad.

Shares of NIO (NIO) rose 2.1% in Wednesday morning trading.

data.title: AllianceBernstein closes Arya amid mounting pressure on multistrategy hedge fund challengers - report
data.description:
AllianceBernstein (AB) has shut its multi-manager fund AB Arya, a part of the asset manager's $6B hedge fund platform, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday.

A total of 95% of the liquidation proceeds are anticipated to be made available for distribution to clients in June, according to a statement sent to the media house.

AB Arya's closure is another sign of pressure on multistrategy hedge fund challengers after Eisler Capital shut in 2025, according to the report.

data.title: Google Cloud inks deal with Thinking Machines Labs to provide Nvidia-powered AI infrastructure
data.description:
Google Cloud (GOOG)(GOOGL) has inked a new agreement with Thinking Machines Labs to provide the startup with expanded access to its Nvidia-powered (NVDA) AI infrastructure.

Thinking Machines will utilize A4X Max with Blackwell architecture through Google Cloud, becoming one of the first Google Cloud customers to utilize Nvidia's GB300 NVL72. 

The San Francisco-based AI startup was co-founded last year by Mira Murati, a former chief technology officer at OpenAI. Its flagship product, Tinker, helps users automate the creation of custom, frontier AI models.

"By leveraging A4X Max and the AI Hypercomputer integrated stack, Google Cloud got us running at record speed with the reliability we demand," said Myle Ott, a founding researcher at Thinking Machines. "This seamless integration of high-performance compute, fast storage, GKE orchestration, and automated remediation via Cluster Director has allowed us to focus on the unique aspects of the stack, like Tinker and reinforcement learning."

"Through this new agreement and our deep partnership with Nvidia, we'll help Thinking Machines accelerate even further, using Google Cloud's AI Hypercomputer, which brings together purpose-built hardware, open software, and flexible consumption models in an optimized architecture," said Mark Lohmeyer, general manager of AI and Computing Infrastructure at Google Cloud. 

Although terms of the deal were not revealed, it was a multi-billion dollar agreement, according to TechCrunch.

data.title: Business inflation expectations continue to tick up, the Atlanta Fed says
data.description:
Year-ahead business inflation expectations: +2.3% vs. +2.1% in March, according to a survey the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta released on Wednesday.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: MongoDB appoints two product chiefs for AI and core products
data.description:
MongoDB (MDB) said on Wednesday it hired Pablo Stern as chief product officer for AI and emerging products and named Ben Cefalo chief product officer for core products.

The executives will report to CEO CJ Desai, while Jim Scharf will remain chief technology officer.

Stern previously held senior roles at ServiceNow (NOW), where he helped scale its IT operations management business, MongoDB said. Stern will oversee the AI product portfolio, including Search, Vector Search, and Voyage, as well as future AI initiatives.

Cefalo, who joined MongoDB in 2017, will lead the company’s core products, including Atlas and Enterprise Advanced.

"Having a dedicated leader for each focus area will allow us to deliver what our customers need as they standardize both their core applications and AI workloads on MongoDB," said CEO Desai.

Stock up +2.9% in early trading.

data.title: Certara to sell regulatory and medical writing business for up to $135M
data.description:
Certara (CERT) shares gained on Thursday after the provider of drug development solutions announced an agreement to sell its regulatory and medical writing business to Veristat, a contract research organization, in a deal worth up to $135M.

CEO Jon Resnick said that the sale will enable Certara (CERT) to focus on its model-informed drug development and clinical intelligence solutions. “This transaction underlines our commitment to the acceleration of AI-integrated modeling and simulation across the drug development lifecycle,” he added.

The unit, with nearly 220 employees, generated $50M in revenue and $17M in adjusted EBITDA excluding overheads last year. Certara (CERT) plans to revise its full-year outlook once the deal finalizes in Q2 2026.

“Consistent with our strategy, we intend to deploy the proceeds to drive long-term value for customers, patients, and shareholders,” Resnick said.

data.title: Teleflex gains on report PE firms teamed up for takeover offer
data.description:
Teleflex (TFX) rose 9.7% after a report that CVC Capital Partners and GTCR  teamed up for a takeover offer for the medical equipment provider. 

The PE firms have been working together to consider a possible takeover for Teleflex (TFX), according to a  Bloomberg report late Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

It wasn't clear if Teleflex would be receptive to a takeout and there's no guarantee that an approach will lead to a transaction, according to the report. 

CVC, GTCR and Teleflex declined to comment to Bloomberg.

A potential offer comes as activist investor Irenic Capital Management last month sent a letter to the medical device maker urging the company to explore strategic alternatives, including a potential buyout, and revamp its board.

data.description:
